chunk_summary: This section focuses on distinguishing non-response from adherence failure in feeding-related medical conditions, emphasizing targeted investigation and evaluation strategies within pediatric and celiac disease management frameworks.
learning_objectives: Understand the difference between non-response and adherence failure in feeding-related conditions.
Learn targeted investigation methods for pediatric TB and celiac disease management.
Explore guidelines for diagnosing and managing celiac disease in India.
Analyze adherence challenges in pediatric feeding interventions.
key_facts: The Paediatric TB Management Guideline (2022) provides specific strategies for evaluating feeding-related adherence issues.
Celiac disease management guidelines by ICMR emphasize diagnostic protocols tailored for Indian populations.
Non-response in feeding interventions may stem from physiological factors rather than adherence failure.
Targeted investigations are critical for distinguishing adherence failure from true non-response.
Indian Council of Medical Research guidelines are a key resource for celiac disease management in India.
reference_quote: Guideline for diagnosis and management of celiac disease in India — https://www.icmr.gov.in/icmrobject/custom_data/pdf/resourceguidelines/ICMR%20-%20Diagnosis%20and%20Managmemnt.pdf
document_key_topics: Adherence failure in pediatric TB management
chunk_heading: FEEDING;NONRESPONSE EVALUATION AND TARGETED INVESTIGATION;DISTINGUISHING NON-RESPONSE FROM ADHERENCE FAILURE;
